title: Sindhu ends two-year drought, wins maiden Japan Open title
indian_angle: PV Sindhu, a prominent Indian shuttler, has ended her title drought by winning a Super 750 event, a significant boost for Indian badminton.
summary: PV Sindhu defeated Akane Yamaguchi in straight games to claim her first Japan Open title, marking a triumphant return to form after a two-year wait.

title: Gopichand on Sindhu's comeback: "She can beat top players"
indian_angle: National coach Pullela Gopichand's endorsement highlights the renewed potential of India's Olympic medallist PV Sindhu.
summary: Pullela Gopichand expressed confidence in PV Sindhu's ability to defeat top players, remarking on her recent surge in form and potential for future success.

title: Major venue revamp for World Championships in Delhi
indian_angle: India is hosting the BWF World Championships 2026, and significant infrastructure improvements are underway to ensure a world-class event.
summary: Following issues at the India Open, a major venue revamp is planned for the BWF World Championships 2026 in Delhi, addressing concerns like bird droppings and stray animals.

title: Gopichand "shocked" by badminton's Commonwealth Games exclusion
indian_angle: Pullela Gopichand, a prominent Indian coach, expresses concern over the sport's absence from a major multi-sport event important for Indian athletes.
summary: Pullela Gopichand voiced his surprise and disappointment regarding badminton's exclusion from the upcoming Commonwealth Games, highlighting its importance for athletes.

title: BAI implements 3x15 scoring for junior domestic tournaments
indian_angle: This new scoring system will significantly impact the development and competitive experience of young Indian shuttlers.
summary: The Badminton Association of India has introduced a 3x15 scoring system for U-13, U-15, and U-17 domestic ranking tournaments and national championships.

title: Chennai to host Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Tournament
indian_angle: This tournament is a key event for senior Indian shuttlers to improve their national rankings and prepare for higher-level competitions.
summary: The Yonex-Sunrise All India Senior Ranking Badminton Tournament is scheduled to take place in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, from July 16th to 23rd, 2026.
